description_long: This obelisk is a compact, roughly hewn stone monument set atop a multi-layered, irregular stone base. The shaft of the obelisk is short and rectangular, with a textured surface that suggests it was carved from a single block of stone. The base consists of two main tiers: a smaller, blocky upper platform and a larger, uneven lower platform that appears to be constructed from rough, natural stones. The entire structure has a weathered, ancient appearance, with visible chisel marks and a rugged, unfinished texture. There are no visible inscriptions or decorative carvings, giving the monument a stark and utilitarian look. The obelisk would likely serve as a small commemorative marker or boundary stone in an urban park or plaza.
description: A small, rugged stone obelisk with a textured shaft and a multi-tiered, irregular stone base. The structure has a weathered, ancient appearance and lacks visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rectangular shaft rising from a blocky upper base, with the uneven lower platform clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights the rough texture of the shaft and the layered construction of the base, with more of the right side of the lower platform exposed.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the irregular, natural stonework of the lower platform and the back face of the shaft.
description_view_3: This side view emphasizes the thickness of the shaft and the asymmetrical, sloped nature of the base.
attribute_base_design: The base is composed of two main tiers: an upper rectangular block and a larger, irregular lower platform made of rough, natural stone. The lower platform is uneven and appears to be constructed from several stones, giving the base a rugged, organic look.
attribute_tip_design: The tip of the obelisk is flat and unadorned, with a rough, chiseled finish matching the rest of the shaft.
